items.title: Kiwis urged to get naturing this Conservation Week
items.content: Conservation Week/ Te Wiki o Te Taiao kicks off on April 20, with hundreds of events happening across the country and a simple call to all New Zealanders: get naturing.

items.title: New bridge underway for Tongariro River Trail
items.content: Progress is coming to the Tongariro River Trail, with construction of a new suspension bridge - right next to the existing Major Jones bridge - due to begin on Monday 20 April.

items.title: Census shows jump in the population of our rarest endemic bird
items.content: If you’re out naturing on Northland or Auckland’s beaches, spotting a tara iti (NZ fairy tern) is like finding a needle in a haystack.

items.title: Spectacular new Hooker Valley bridge to open later this year
items.content: The massive new suspension bridge being built up the Hooker Valley at Aoraki/Mount Cook will open in late July/early August this year.

items.title: Orca incident prompts call for information
items.content: Enjoy the wonder of orca but please keep your distance.

items.title: Hunters on target at police, DOC forest checkpoint
items.content: DOC and NZ Police say they’re encouraged by the sensible behaviour, positive response and correct paperwork they encountered during a pre-Easter hunting compliance operation.

items.title: Watch for wild dogs in Waioweka
items.content: DOC is calling for hunters to report sightings of a pack of feral dogs spotted recently in Waioweka Conservation Area.

items.title: DOC statement: Outcome of Wildlife Act investigation
items.content: Read DOC's statement re: Outcome of Wildlife Act investigation focused on stony corals.

items.title: Rat detections on predator free island highlights importance of biosecurity network
items.content: A second rat incursion on predator free Ulva Island this year highlights the value of the island's strong biosecurity surveillance network.

items.title: Historic viaducts closed for safety, Hump Ridge Track remains open
items.content: People out naturing in Southern Fiordland this long weekend should be aware of the ongoing closure of three historic structures.

items.title: Clean gear to stop weed spread on Easter outings
items.content: Those heading out naturing over Easter are asked to check and clean their gear before getting into the hills, to stop the spread of invasive weeds.

items.title: Kapiti takes aim at 'possums of freshwater'
items.content: A Kapiti Coast catchment is well on the way to becoming free of koi carp, a noxious introduced fish that leaves a trail of destruction.

items.title: DOC hut with ‘best view in New Zealand’ gets a makeover
items.content: The iconic Mueller Hut in the Aoraki/Mount Cook National Park has had a spruce up, which is no easy feat.

items.title: New Zealand leads protection of world’s rarest seabirds
items.content: Some of the world’s rarest seabirds have a new level of international protection following adoption of a New Zealand-led proposal under the Convention on Migratory Species.

items.title: Kiwi caught on camera a Pureora milestone
items.content: A rare kiwi has been captured on a trail camera in the northern Pureora Forest's Waipapa block – the first recorded sighting of the species in the area for more than 25 years.

items.title: DOC investigates black-billed gull deaths
items.content: DOC is appealing for information on the poisoning of threatened and protected native gulls on the Te Anau lakefront this summer.

items.title: AI traps for Heaphy a first for DOC
items.content: An AI trap network being set up around huts on the Heaphy Track will reduce both pest numbers and time spent maintaining traps on this iconic New Zealand walk.

items.title: Illegal fishing spikes in marine reserve, public tips welcomed
items.content: DOC staff say illegal fishing activity this summer in Te Pātaka o Rākaihautū/Banks Peninsula’s Pōhatu marine reserve is the highest in 10 years.

items.title: Ready, set, naturing: DOC Great Walk, hut and campsite bookings open from 12 May
items.content: Dust off the tramping boots and warm up those clicking fingers - bookings for DOC's Great Walks, huts and campsites for the 2026/27 season open from 12 May.

items.title: Hunters asked to watch for weeds this roar
items.content: Hunters operating in New Zealand's remote places during the roar are being asked to keep an eye out for invasive weeds and report any sightings, and to clean their gear before heading out.

items.title: Wellington Te Whanganui-a-Tara to lead next phase of Predator Free 2050
items.content: Wellington Te Whanganui-a-Tara is set to become New Zealand’s first predator-free city, as the Government steps up the next phase of Predator Free 2050, Conservation Minister Tama Potaka says.

items.title: Endangered kakī/black stilts gobble up hearts
items.content: One of the world’s rarest wading birds, the kakī/black stilt, is feeling the love from one of New Zealand’s largest red meat processors, Alliance Group.

items.title: Competitive allocation process applications closed for NZ's largest farm
items.content: The application period for potential operators wanting to run commercial farming in Molesworth Recreation Reserve has closed.

items.title: Mangakara Nature Walk to reopen, caution urged
items.content: The popular Mangakara Nature Walk in Pirongia Forest Park has reopened today as DOC continues clean-up, repair and assessment work after the wild storm that swept across Waikato in February.

items.title: Last chance to save globally rare plants from rabbits
items.content: An ambitious plan to reduce rabbits and hares inside a DOC scientific reserve near Christchurch to protect rare native plants begins today with a targeted bait laying operation using helicopters.

items.title: Rāhui declared following death on Pouākai Range
items.content: Ka tipare a Taranaki ki te raukawakawa, he tohu aroha nui ki te iwi kua ngaro.

items.title: Allocation process for flight landings in Westland Tai Poutini National Park opens
items.content: Applications for the Westland Tai Poutini National Park aircraft landings allocation process have opened and will close 5 pm Friday 24 April 2026.

items.title: DOC and Meridian Energy celebrate 10-years of powering Kākāpō Recovery
items.content: DOC and Meridian Energy have renewed a longstanding partnership supporting the world-leading Kākāpō Recovery Programme for a further three years.

items.title: Call for proposals to restore the historic Chateau Tongariro
items.content: Proposals are being sought from experienced operators to restore and operate the historic Chateau Tongariro and its associated buildings, Conservation Minister Tama Potaka says.

items.title: Coromandel brown kiwi move off Motutapu for the first time
